strategic_overview: The problem is best viewed as a labeled uncrossing or rewriting cube complex. In dimension 0, the cells are exactly noncrossing inverse-letter foldings of the freely trivial word, equivalently valid plane trees; the deep-read material gives a strong normal form here: the greedy folding exists, the valid local-move graph is connected, and it has a unique sink/source depending on orientation. The hard part is not the existence of foldings, but extending this 0- and 1-dimensional structure to all crossing cells while respecting the region-label condition and isotopy classes of immersed diagrams.

directions.name: Primary route: greedy-folding discrete Morse collapse
directions.description: Try to prove that F_w collapses to the greedy noncrossing folding. First identify 0-cells with w-valid plane trees and 1-cells with the valid local moves of Condon-Heitsch-Hoos. Then extend the type-2 orientation toward the greedy tree to a discrete Morse matching on higher cubes: for each noncritical cell, choose the first local non-greedy feature and pair the cell with the cell obtained by adding or deleting the corresponding crossing coordinate. The target proof is collapsibility, hence contractibility, with the greedy folding as the only critical cell.
key_ideas: Use the greedy stack folding as the canonical base vertex.
Use the type-2 local-move orientation as a Lyapunov gradient on vertices.
For higher cells, use a leftmost or first-deviation rule analogous to Squier/Farley collapsing schemes.
Exploit quasi-reducedness to rule out overlapping cancellation ambiguities of the form ell ell^{-1} ell.
relevant_techniques: Valid Plane Trees: Combinatorial Models for RNA Secondary Structures with Watson-Crick Base Pairs, https://arxiv.org/abs/1501.03238, Proposition 2.6-2.7 gives the exact four-letter criterion for a valid local move; this should be the local model for every 1-cell of F_w.
Valid Plane Trees, https://arxiv.org/abs/1501.03238, Theorem 3.4 and Corollaries 3.5, 3.8, 3.10 give the greedy folding, unique sink, acyclicity, and directed paths to the greedy tree.
k-Foldability of Words, https://arxiv.org/abs/1710.10616, Basic equivalence in Section 2 says freely trivial words are precisely foldable words, so F_w has at least one 0-cell.

Diagram groups and directed 2-complexes: homotopy and homology, https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0301225, Theorem 9.2 gives the leftmost-special-term collapsing scheme for Squier complexes; it is a good template for pairing higher-dimensional cells of F_w.

The cancellation norm and the geometry of bi-invariant word metrics, https://www.cambridge.org/core/services/aop-cambridge-core/content/view/BCA930D8BD51C5DCD2828E93216C40C9/S0017089515000129a.pdf/div-class-title-the-cancellation-norm-and-the-geometry-of-bi-invariant-word-metrics-div.pdf, Lemma 2.10 gives the first-letter inverse-occurrence split for free-group cancellations, useful for inductive control of the greedy target.
directions.potential_obstacles: The main technical gap is extending the vertex orientation to cubes. A k-crossing matching may not be transparently a set of k independent valid tree rotations until one proves a precise local dictionary. One must also prove that the chosen first bad crossing always gives another admissible cell of F_w and that the resulting Morse matching is acyclic.

directions.name: Squier or Farley CAT(0) model for free cancellation
directions.description: Build a directed 2-complex or Squier-type cube complex whose vertices are cancellation states or reduction diagrams for w, and whose cubes are simultaneous independent local cancellation moves. Then prove F_w is the universal cover or a convex subcomplex of that Squier complex. If successful, contractibility follows from Farley-type CAT(0) or universal-cover contractibility theorems.
key_ideas: Interpret a crossing as a pending binary choice between two local cancellation orders.
Use cubes for commuting independent cancellation choices.
Treat quasi-reducedness as the condition excluding overlapping reductions ell ell^{-1} ell.
Aim for a universal-cover model, not merely a quotient Squier complex, because ordinary Squier complexes need not be contractible.
relevant_techniques: Diagram groups and directed 2-complexes: homotopy and homology, https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0301225, Theorem 8.5 proves the universal cover of a Squier complex is contractible.

Diagram groups and directed 2-complexes, https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0301225, Lemma 3.7 realizes Squier cubes as positive paths through 2-cells; this closely matches the interval coordinates in the definition of C(M).
An introduction to diagram groups, https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.12068, Theorem 4.9 proves that the diagram graph M(P,w) is median and its cube completion is CAT(0), hence contractible.

Groupoids and the Algebra of Rewriting in Group Presentations, https://arxiv.org/abs/1901.04348, Definition 3.1 and Theorem 3.2 show how group-presentation relation applications with disjoint contexts commute via Squier squares.

An introduction to diagram groups, https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.12068, Section 5.3 caveat warns that monoid relations of the form u=1 can break nonpositive curvature; this is exactly why quasi-reducedness and a directed-2-complex rather than naive monoid presentation should be used.
directions.potential_obstacles: Free cancellation uses empty-word relations, which are dangerous in ordinary semigroup Squier theory. Also, crossings in F_w are geometric and may correspond to nested rather than disjoint word contexts. The key test is whether every k-cell of F_w can be encoded as a thin diagram with k independent 2-cells and whether the region-label condition is exactly the Squier legality condition.

directions.name: Face-poset and shellability approach via uncrossing orders
directions.description: Compare F_w with the full uncrossing poset of matchings. Prove first that every admissible crossing matching is lensless and that resolving crossings gives an interval or induced subcomplex inside the full uncrossing CW poset. Then try to show the label-compatible part is shellable or collapsible, possibly using a recursive atom/coatom order based on the greedy folding.
key_ideas: Use full uncrossing posets as the ambient regular CW model.
Characterize admissible cells as those whose complete resolutions are valid foldings of w.
Use the greedy folding to choose a recursive coatom order on the label-compatible subposet.
If the label-compatible subposet is an interval, order ideal, or shellable ball, contractibility follows.
relevant_techniques: Shellability of face posets of electrical networks and the CW poset property, https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.06217, Theorem 3.18 and Corollary 3.19 prove the full uncrossing poset is shellable and a CW poset.

Generalized Recursive Atom Ordering and Equivalence to CL-Shellability, https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.03949, Theorem 7.1 upgrades uncrossing posets to dual CL-shellable; its unique-earliest-label idea is a natural labeling strategy for admissible resolutions.

Circular Planar Electrical Networks I: The Electrical Poset EPn, https://arxiv.org/abs/1309.2697, Theorem 2.4.2 and Proposition 2.4.3 identify critical diagrams with lensless medial graphs and show how motions reduce dependence on drawings.
Electroid Varieties and a Compactification of the Space of Electrical Networks, https://arxiv.org/abs/1402.6261, Lemma 4.12 gives an endpoint criterion for when an uncrossing remains lensless.

The cd-indices of intervals in the uncrossing partial order on matchings, https://www.combinatorics.org/ojs/index.php/eljc/article/download/v26i1p40/pdf/, Theorem 10 and Theorem 28 give an affine-Bruhat encoding and product-plus-zipping interval recursion.
Subword Complexes in Coxeter Groups, https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0309259, Theorems 2.5 and 3.7 give a model for proving a deletion-choice complex is vertex-decomposable and hence a ball or sphere.
directions.potential_obstacles: The admissible label condition may not define an order ideal or interval in the full uncrossing poset. Also, the electrical-network literature often quotients lensless diagrams by Yang-Baxter motions, whereas F_w uses isotopy classes of actual immersed interval diagrams. Inherited shellability of an arbitrary induced subcomplex is not automatic.

directions.name: Hatcher-style surgery to the star of the greedy matching
directions.description: Fix the greedy noncrossing matching T0. Put each crossing matching in minimal position relative to T0 and perform ordered surgeries that reduce intersections with T0, imitating Hatcher's contraction of arc complexes to the star of a fixed arc. The desired deformation retracts F_w to the cubical star of the greedy vertex.
key_ideas: Use T0 as the reference object.
Order intersections along arcs of T0 and remove them one at a time.
Make each surgery cellular by moving linearly inside a cube or adjacent cube.
Use interval decompositions along greedy arcs to reduce validity checks to smaller subwords.
relevant_techniques: Triangulations of Surfaces, https://pi.math.cornell.edu/~hatcher/Papers/TriangSurf.pdf, the surgery deformation retraction onto the star of a fixed arc is the direct geometric template.

Triangulations of Surfaces, https://pi.math.cornell.edu/~hatcher/Papers/TriangSurf.pdf, the minimal-position isotopy fact is relevant because operations on F_w must be well defined on isotopy classes.

Triangulations of Surfaces, https://pi.math.cornell.edu/~hatcher/Papers/TriangSurf.pdf, the link-after-cutting identification suggests how fixing a matched arc should split F_w into smaller word problems.
Valid Plane Trees, https://arxiv.org/abs/1501.03238, Proposition 2.8 says validity is inherited by interval subtrees after cutting along a common edge.
directions.potential_obstacles: Surgery on arbitrary arc systems can discard boundary-parallel or duplicate arcs, but F_w consists of perfect matchings with fixed endpoints; careless surgery may leave the class of matchings. The star of a full matching vertex is also subtler than the star of a single arc, so the target subcomplex must be defined carefully.

directions.name: Longer-shot positive-geometry realization
directions.description: Try to encode the letter labels as color or linear constraints on Lam's electrical-network compactification or its positive Grassmannian slice. If F_w can be realized as a cell closure, link, or positive slice preserved by a contractive flow, then existing ball theorems would imply contractibility, possibly even a closed-ball structure.
key_ideas: Use medial strands of electrical networks as the unlabeled version of the crossing matchings.
Translate inverse-letter compatibility into boundary-color constraints on medial regions or grove coordinates.
Seek a positive slice or cell closure whose face stratification is exactly F_w.
Apply contractive-flow or regularity theorems rather than constructing a combinatorial collapse.
relevant_techniques: Electroid Varieties and a Compactification of the Space of Electrical Networks, https://arxiv.org/abs/1402.6261, Propositions 4.10-4.11 and Theorem 5.8 give a compactification decomposed into cells indexed by matchings.
Electroid Varieties, https://arxiv.org/abs/1402.6261, Proposition 5.26 identifies closure order with uncrossing order.

The Totally Nonnegative Grassmannian is a Ball, https://arxiv.org/abs/1707.02010, Lemma 2.3 gives the contractive-flow ball criterion and Theorem 1.3 proves Lam's electrical compactification is a closed ball.

Regularity theorem for totally nonnegative flag varieties, https://arxiv.org/abs/1904.00527, Theorem 2.4 gives an abstract regularity criterion using shellability plus Fomin-Shapiro local product charts.
directions.potential_obstacles: This is probably too heavy for a first proof. The known positive spaces index cells by endpoint matchings modulo electrical equivalence, while F_w has extra word labels and actual isotopy-class data. It is unclear that the label-compatible locus is semialgebraic, convex, positive, or flow-invariant.

key_obstacles: The central barrier is to find the right combinatorial model for a k-cell. The 0-cell theory is well understood through valid plane trees, and the 1-cell theory should be the valid local-move criterion, but contractibility depends on higher-dimensional compatibility of several crossings. One must prove that the region-label condition is stable under all face maps, that it excludes lens or overlap pathologies, and that quasi-reducedness is used exactly to rule out ambiguous overlapping cancellations. A second barrier is the quotient issue: uncrossing posets and electrical networks usually identify diagrams up to Yang-Baxter motions, while F_w is built from isotopy classes of immersed intervals. A third barrier is regularity: even if the face poset looks shellable, the attaching maps in F_w must be controlled well enough for poset topology or discrete Morse theory to apply.
recommended_starting_point: Start with the greedy-folding Morse route. The very first step should be a local dictionary lemma: prove that 0-cells of F_w are exactly w-valid plane trees, and that 1-cells of F_w are exactly the valid local moves characterized in Valid Plane Trees, Proposition 2.6-2.7, https://arxiv.org/abs/1501.03238. Immediately after that, prove the 2-cell compatibility lemma: two valid local moves span a square of F_w precisely when they can be performed independently, with the remaining three-edge cases matching the rank-two uncrossing analyses in Shellability of face posets of electrical networks, Lemma 4.1 and Proposition 4.3, https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.06217. If these two lemmas work cleanly, the discrete Morse collapse to the greedy folding is the most direct and problem-specific path to contractibility.
